seed_instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days.
org_constraint: Ensure the email avoids technical jargon and is easy to understand.
new_constraint: The email must include at least one technical term related to the project.
instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days. Ensure the email avoids technical jargon and is easy to understand. The email must include at least one technical term related to the project.
response:
Subject: Request for Extension: [Project Name] – [Your Full Name]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I hope this email finds you well.

I am writing to respectfully ask for a short extension on our upcoming project, which is due in two days. I have been working diligently on the assignment, but I have encountered some unexpected challenges that are taking longer than anticipated to resolve.

Specifically, I am currently facing some issues with the **data visualization** component of the project. I want to ensure that the charts I create accurately represent the data without any misleading trends, and I need a little more time to refine this section to meet the high standards of your course.

I am confident that I can complete the project to a high quality by [New Proposed Date, e.g., next Friday]. I would greatly appreciate the opportunity to submit my work on that date instead.

Thank you very much for your time and understanding. Please let me know if this arrangement works for you.

Best regards,

[Your Full Name]  
[Your Student ID Number]  
[Course Name/Number]
evaluation_result:

- "Specifically, I am currently facing some issues with the **data visualization** component of the project."

- Instruction 2 requires the email to avoid technical jargon and be easy to understand. The inclusion of the term "data visualization" is considered technical jargon, which violates this constraint.  

**Step 3**: 1
